30.reason: [Error: p1[i].exponent is used before p1[i] is fully read; i is incremented first, so p1[i] reads the next uninitialized element. Should be &p1[i].coefficient, &p1[i].exponent with i incremented after both reads, or use p1[i-1].exponent.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: p1[i].exponent is assigned to the next element (i+1) instead of p1[i].exponent. Should be &p1[i].exponent, but i is incremented before reading exponent, so it reads into the wrong index.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: exponent is stored at index i after increment, causing off-by-one; should store coefficient and exponent at same index before increment.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Bug: i is incremented before reading exponent, causing exponent to be stored in the next element. Also, the loop condition using getchar() is unreliable.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  i++ increments the index before reading the exponent, causing the coefficient and exponent to be stored in different array elements.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Index mismatch: 'i' is incremented before being used for exponent, so exponent is stored at p1[i] while coefficient is at p1[i-1]]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Index mismatch: after i++, the exponent is stored at p1[i] instead of p1[i-1], causing misalignment between coefficient and exponent]

55.reason: [Error: Using continue skips the printf for the current term, but after merging, the term at index m should not be printed. However, the logic is flawed because it does not handle the case where multiple consecutive terms have the same exponent correctly, and it may print terms that should have been merged.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: Using continue skips the printf for the current term, but after merging, the term at index m should not be printed. However, the logic is flawed because it does not handle the case where multiple consecutive terms have the same exponent correctly, and it may print terms that should have been merged.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: merging logic only combines into next term, but current term's coefficient is not zeroed; also with three or more equal exponents, earlier terms remain non-zero and are never printed.]

59.reason: [Error: This prints the last term unconditionally, but if the last term was combined with a previous term (i.e., its coefficient was updated), it may still print a zero-coefficient term or miss combining. Also, the loop above uses continue which skips printing combined terms, but the last term is printed here without checking if it should be combined with a previous term.]

26.reason: p3 array size is too small; the product of two polynomials can have up to num1*num2 terms, which can easily exceed 100. Should be increased to at least 20000.
